title: ECB staff macroeconomic projections for the euro area, March 2026
summary: The ECB staff macroeconomic projections for March 2026 show the euro area economy remained resilient in 2025 but faces renewed uncertainty from the Middle East conflict, with energy price shocks expected to push HICP inflation to 3.1% in Q2 2026 before declining. The baseline projects GDP growth of 0.9% in 2026, 1.3% in 2027, and 1.4% in 2028, with headline HICP inflation of 2.6%, 2.0%, and 2.1% respectively.
key_points: Baseline GDP growth: 0.9% (2026), 1.3% (2027), 1.4% (2028); revised down 0.3pp for 2026 and 0.1pp for 2027 from December 2025 projections
Baseline HICP inflation: 2.6% (2026), 2.0% (2027), 2.1% (2028); revised up 0.7pp for 2026 versus December projections
Energy price baseline assumes oil peaks at ~USD 90/barrel and gas at ~€50/MWh in Q2 2026, then declines
Adverse scenario assumes oil peaks at USD 119/barrel and gas at €87/MWh in Q2 2026, with inflation 0.9pp higher in 2026
Severe scenario assumes oil peaks at USD 145/barrel and gas at €106/MWh, with inflation 1.8pp higher in 2026 and 2.8pp higher in 2027
EU ETS2 implementation expected to add 0.2pp to headline inflation in 2028
why_it_matters: These projections inform ECB monetary policy decisions and provide market participants with the institution's baseline view on growth and inflation trajectories under energy price uncertainty from the Middle East conflict. The three scenarios (baseline, adverse, severe) quantify the range of potential outcomes depending on energy price trajectory.
macro_context: The Middle East war has disrupted shipping through the Strait of Hormuz, pushing energy prices higher and creating near-term inflationary pressure. While domestic demand remains the main growth driver supported by resilient labor markets and government spending (particularly in Germany), external factors including export competitiveness challenges and tariffs on US exports weigh on the outlook. Wage growth is expected to moderate but slower than previously anticipated due to inflation compensation effects.

issues_found: Source body was truncated in processing; adverse and severe scenario GDP impact details may be incomplete, but key inflation and growth figures are fully supported in visible text

human_review.notes: Source contains important macro catalyst data for ECB policy outlook. Verify published_at date (appears to be March 2026 based on content discussing Q1 2026 data and Q2 2026 forecasts). Three scenarios provide useful reference for risk analysis.

notes: This is an ECB staff research publication containing macroeconomic projections for the euro area. Although SOURCE METADATA TARGET field indicates 'market structure', the content is exclusively macroeconomic analysis focused on GDP growth, inflation trajectories, and energy price scenarios under the Middle East conflict. Classified as macro catalysts. The three hypothetical scenarios (baseline, adverse, severe) provide policymakers and market participants with a framework for understanding the uncertainty around energy price shocks. No market structure or operational details discussed.
